description: Site-level navigation with optional mega-menu content panels. Radix renders a <nav> landmark; triggers are <button>s with aria-expanded and aria-controls pointing at their content panels. NavigationMenuLink is a plain <a> you can mark as aria-current="page" for the active route.

keyboard.action: Moves focus to / from the menu (and into open content panels).
Opens the focused trigger's content panel.
Moves focus within an open content panel.
Closes the open panel and returns focus to its trigger.
Moves focus to the first / last top-level trigger.

focus.notes: Unlike Menubar, focus is not scoped inside the open panel; users can Tab out of the menu at any point. Radix restores focus to the trigger when the panel is closed via Escape.

announcements: The root announces as a nav landmark.
Each trigger announces as a button with its expanded state.
Links inside panels announce normally; consumers should mark the current page with aria-current="page".
screenReader.notes: The ChevronDown icon inside NavigationMenuTrigger is aria-hidden so it isn't read as "down arrow"; the trigger's expanded state is conveyed via aria-expanded.

contrast.text: Trigger text and links use foreground; hover / focus / open states swap to text-accent-foreground on bg-accent. All meet WCAG AA
contrast.ui: Indicator arrow and viewport border use border token; meet AA non-text contrast

motion.reducedMotion: Viewport and indicator animate with data-[motion] / data-[state] slide + fade + zoom utilities. Gate with motion-reduce:animate-none if strict compliance matters.

consumerResponsibilities: Mark the active route with aria-current="page" on the relevant NavigationMenuLink; Radix does not infer it.
Use NavigationMenu for site navigation; use Menubar for app-style menu bars and DropdownMenu for per-element action menus.
Keep top-level labels short; the mega-menu panel is where detail belongs.
